text.title: A/B Testing Ads Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of A/B Testing Ads. Each pairs symptom + sourced root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Test changes hook + image + CTA; variant wins 18% lift; team cannot attribute lift.
antipattern.fix: Re-run as three sequential single-variable tests.

antipattern.symptom: Test stopped at day 3 when variant 'looked' ahead; subsequent runs show null effect.
antipattern.root-cause: sample-size-pre-calc rule violated; early-stop without sequential testing math.
antipattern.fix: Pre-calc sample size; commit to full window; use sequential testing math if early-stop needed.

antipattern.symptom: Team A/B tests 5 CTA variants without testing offer; lift never exceeds 3%.
antipattern.root-cause: priority-order rule violated; low-leverage variable tested first.
antipattern.fix: Test offer first; CTA last; expect 20-40% lift on offer tests.

antipattern.symptom: Test concluded with 12% lift; 6 months later team retests same variant; verdict not recorded.
antipattern.fix: Wire learnings DB; require verdict + sample-size + learnings per test.
